title: Hill Forts of Rajasthan
tagline: Hill Forts of Rajasthan — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: The Hill Forts of Rajasthan are a group of six historic forts located across Rajasthan state in northwestern India.
overview.description: The Hill Forts of Rajasthan are a group of six historic forts located across Rajasthan state in northwestern India. They have been clustered as a series and designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2013. The hill forts series include—Chittor Fort at Chittorgarh, Kumbhalgarh Fort at Rajsamand, Ranthambore Fort at Sawai Madhopur, Gagron Fort at Jhalawar, Amer Fort at Jaipur and Jaisalmer Fort at Jaisalmer. Rajasthan has over one hundred fortifications on hills and mountainous terrain.

topPlaces.name: Kalika Mata Temple, Chittorgarh Fort
topPlaces.description: Kalika Mata Temple is an 8th-century Hindu temple located within the Chittor Fort in the Chittorgarh municipality of Rajasthan state in India. It was originally a Sun temple which was partly destroyed in sack of Chittor, it was rebuilt during the time of Rana Kumbha.

topPlaces.name: Samadhishvara Temple, Chittorgarh
topPlaces.description: The Samadhishvara Temple (IAST: Samādhīśvara) is a Hindu temple located in the Chittor Fort in Rajasthan, India. It is dedicated to Shiva, who is called "Samadhishvara", that is, "Lord of Samadhi".

topPlaces.name: Vijaya Stambha
topPlaces.description: The Vijaya Stambha is a Rajput victory monument located within Chittor Fort in Chittorgarh, Rajasthan, India. The tower was constructed by the Hindu Rajput king Rana Kumbha of Mewar in 1448 CE to commemorate his victory over the army of Sultanate of Malwa led by Mahmud Khilji in the Battle of Sarangpur.

topPlaces.name: Kirti Stambha
topPlaces.description: Kirti Stambha is a tower situated at Chittor Fort in Chittorgarh town of Rajasthan, India. It has been a major Jain center since at least the Kushana period, producing scholars such as Acharya Haribhadrasuri in the 6th century and Virasena in the 9th.

topPlaces.name: Chittorgarh
topPlaces.description: Chittorgarh (Hindi pronunciation: [t͡ʃɪt̪ːɔːɽ.gəɽʱ] ; also Chitror or Chittor or Chittaurgarh) is a major city in the state of Rajasthan in western India. It lies on the Berach River, a tributary of the Banas, and is the administrative headquarters of Chittorgarh District.

topPlaces.name: Bhojunda Stromatolite Park
topPlaces.description: Bhojunda Stromatolite Park is located near Bhojunda village which is about 6 km south-west of Chittaurgarh city in Rajasthan, India. It is spread across 8 acres on either side of the Chittaurgarh-Udaipur state highway.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Kalika Mata Temple, Chittorgarh Fort & around

items.note: Kalika Mata Temple is an 8th-century Hindu temple located within the Chittor Fort in the Chittorgarh municipality of Rajasthan state in India. It was originally a Sun temple which was partly destroyed in sack of Chittor, it was rebuilt during the time of Rana Kumbha.
The Samadhishvara Temple (IAST: Samādhīśvara) is a Hindu temple located in the Chittor Fort in Rajasthan, India. It is dedicated to Shiva, who is called "Samadhishvara", that is, "Lord of Samadhi".

The Vijaya Stambha is a Rajput victory monument located within Chittor Fort in Chittorgarh, Rajasthan, India. The tower was constructed by the Hindu Rajput king Rana Kumbha of Mewar in 1448 CE to commemorate his victory over the army of Sultanate of Malwa led by Mahmud Khilji in the Battle of Sarangpur.

Kirti Stambha is a tower situated at Chittor Fort in Chittorgarh town of Rajasthan, India. It has been a major Jain center since at least the Kushana period, producing scholars such as Acharya Haribhadrasuri in the 6th century and Virasena in the 9th.

Chittorgarh (Hindi pronunciation: [t͡ʃɪt̪ːɔːɽ.gəɽʱ] ; also Chitror or Chittor or Chittaurgarh) is a major city in the state of Rajasthan in western India. It lies on the Berach River, a tributary of the Banas, and is the administrative headquarters of Chittorgarh District.

Bhojunda Stromatolite Park is located near Bhojunda village which is about 6 km south-west of Chittaurgarh city in Rajasthan, India. It is spread across 8 acres on either side of the Chittaurgarh-Udaipur state highway.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Hill Forts of Rajasthan?
faq.a: The best time to visit Hill Forts of Rajasthan is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Hill Forts of Rajasthan?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Maharana Pratap Airport, Udaipur (81 km) and the nearest railway station is Udaipur City (102 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Hill Forts of Rajasthan?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Hill Forts of Rajasthan at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Hill Forts of Rajasthan?
faq.a: Stays in Hill Forts of Rajasthan start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Hill Forts of Rajasthan?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kalika Mata Temple, Chittorgarh Fort, Samadhishvara Temple, Chittorgarh, Vijaya Stambha, Kirti Stambha, Chittorgarh.
